source: Bahji et al. 2021, Journal of Affective Disorders, PMC7704936

extraction_ground_truth.design: Randomized, placebo-controlled, double-blind crossover trial
extraction_ground_truth.dose: 0.5 mg/kg IV
extraction_ground_truth.primary_efficacy_outcome: Change in 25-item Hamilton Depression Rating Scale within 72 hours after infusion

key_quotes: Seven subjects with major depression completed 2 test days that involved intravenous treatment with ketamine hydrochloride (.5 mg/kg) or saline
first placebo-controlled, double-blinded trial
source_evidence.notes: Crossover design. Each of the 7 subjects received both ketamine and placebo. n_active = n_placebo = 7 (sequence). No phase declared; investigator-initiated mechanism-of-action study. Age criterion not stated in abstract.

extraction_ground_truth.design: Randomized double-blind anesthesia-induction trial in depressed surgical patients
extraction_ground_truth.dose: 1.0 mg/kg IV (induction with propofol + fentanyl + ketamine)
extraction_ground_truth.primary_efficacy_outcome: Hamilton Depression Rating score 1 day after orthopedic surgery

key_quotes: 70 patients with major depression
Group A (n = 35) were induced with propofol, fentanyl, and ketamine and patients in Group B (n = 35) were induced with propofol and fentanyl
source_evidence.notes: Two depressed groups (A vs B); a third non-depressed control group (n=25) was also included but is not part of the depression RCT comparison and is excluded from n_total. Comparator is propofol+fentanyl induction without ketamine; n_placebo holds the comparator-arm count. Age not stated in abstract.

extraction_ground_truth.design: Randomized, placebo-controlled, double-blind crossover trial (NIH Mood Disorders Research Unit)
extraction_ground_truth.dose: 0.5 mg/kg IV over 40 minutes
extraction_ground_truth.primary_efficacy_outcome: Change in 21-item Hamilton Depression Rating Scale at 24 hours post-infusion

key_quotes: Eighteen subjects with DSM-IV major depression (treatment resistant)
After a 2-week drug-free period, subjects were given an intravenous infusion of either ketamine hydrochloride (0.5 mg/kg) or placebo on 2 test days, a week apart
Main Outcome Measure Changes in scores on the primary efficacy measure, the 21-item Hamilton Depression Rating Scale
source_evidence.notes: Crossover. Same 18 subjects randomized to ketamine-then-placebo or placebo-then-ketamine, separated by 1 week washout. n_active = n_placebo = 18 (sequence). Age criterion not in abstract.

extraction_ground_truth.design: Randomized, placebo-controlled, double-blind crossover, add-on trial
extraction_ground_truth.dose: 0.5 mg/kg IV (added to therapeutic levels of lithium or valproate)
extraction_ground_truth.primary_efficacy_outcome: Change in Montgomery-Asberg Depression Rating Scale through 14 days post-infusion

key_quotes: Eighteen subjects with DSM-IV bipolar depression (treatment-resistant)
Subjects maintained at therapeutic levels of lithium or valproate received an intravenous infusion of either ketamine hydrochloride (0.5 mg/kg) or placebo on 2 test days 2 weeks apart
source_evidence.notes: Crossover; same 18 subjects in both arms. n_active = n_placebo = 18 (sequence). Bipolar depression population. Age not in abstract.

extraction_ground_truth.design: Randomized, placebo-controlled, double-blind crossover, add-on trial (replication)
extraction_ground_truth.dose: 0.5 mg/kg IV (added to therapeutic levels of lithium or valproate)
extraction_ground_truth.primary_efficacy_outcome: Change in Montgomery-Asberg Depression Rating Scale through 14 days post-infusion

key_quotes: 15 subjects with DSM-IV bipolar I or II depression
received a single intravenous infusion of either ketamine hydrochloride (0.5 mg/kg) or placebo on two test days two weeks apart
source_evidence.notes: Replication of Diazgranados 2010 design in independent sample. Crossover; n_active = n_placebo = 15. Age not stated in abstract.

extraction_ground_truth.note: This PMID is in Bahji 2021's GT but was classified SECONDARY by our dedup agent (post-hoc analysis of the Zarate 2006 trial focusing on psychotomimetic effects). Extraction GT for the underlying trial is stored under PMID 16894061. Evaluator will skip this entry (no extraction available).

extraction_ground_truth.design: Two-site, parallel-arm, randomized, active-controlled, double-blind trial
extraction_ground_truth.dose: 0.5 mg/kg IV over 40 minutes (single infusion); midazolam 0.045 mg/kg IV as active control
extraction_ground_truth.primary_efficacy_outcome: Change in MADRS depression severity 24 hours after infusion

key_quotes: Patients with treatment-resistant major depression … were randomly assigned under double-blind conditions to receive a single intravenous infusion of ketamine or midazolam in a 2:1 ratio (N=73)
source_evidence.notes: 2:1 randomization of 73 → ketamine 49, midazolam 24 (per published paper). Active comparator is midazolam, not placebo; n_placebo holds the midazolam-arm count. Age criterion ≥18 standard for adult MDD trials.

extraction_ground_truth.design: Randomized, placebo-controlled, double-blind crossover trial
extraction_ground_truth.dose: Intranasal ketamine hydrochloride 50 mg
extraction_ground_truth.primary_efficacy_outcome: Change in MADRS at 24 hours after intranasal dose

key_quotes: Twenty patients with major depression were randomized and 18 completed two treatment days with intranasal ketamine hydrochloride (50 mg) or saline solution in a randomized, double-blind, crossover study
Males and females, aged 21 to 65
source_evidence.notes: Crossover; n_active = n_placebo = n_total = 20 randomized (18 completed). Intranasal route (first crossover RCT of intranasal ketamine for MDD). Age range 21-65 from fulltext.

extraction_ground_truth.design: Randomized, double-blind, placebo-controlled parallel-group trial
extraction_ground_truth.dose: Escitalopram 10 mg/day + single i.v. ketamine 0.5 mg/kg over 40 min vs escitalopram 10 mg/day + saline placebo
extraction_ground_truth.primary_efficacy_outcome: Time to ≥50% MADRS reduction (response) over 4 weeks

key_quotes: Thirty outpatients with severe MDD … were randomized to 4 weeks double-blind treatment with escitalopram 10 mg/day+single-dose i.v. ketamine (0.5 mg/kg over 40 min) or escitalopram 10 mg/day + placebo (0.9% i.v. saline)
By 4 weeks, more escitalopram + ketamine-treated than escitalopram + placebo-treated patients responded (92.3% v. 57.1%)
source_evidence.notes: Combination intervention: ketamine added to newly initiated escitalopram (both groups received escitalopram). Active arm n_active = 13 (response %s imply 13 ket+esc, 14 placebo+esc; 30 randomized minus 3 dropouts = 27 analyzed but n_total = 30 randomized). Age ≥18 standard for adult MDD.

extraction_ground_truth.design: Multicenter, randomized, double-blind, placebo-controlled dose-frequency parallel-group trial
extraction_ground_truth.dose: Ketamine 0.5 mg/kg IV over 40 minutes, twice or thrice weekly for up to 4 weeks; placebo same schedule
extraction_ground_truth.primary_efficacy_outcome: Change in MADRS total score from baseline to day 15

key_quotes: adults (ages 18-64 years) with treatment-resistant depression were randomized to receive either intravenous ketamine (0.5 mg/kg of body weight) or intravenous placebo, administered over 40 minutes, either two or three times weekly, for up to 4 weeks
67 (45 women) of 68 randomized patients received treatment
source_evidence.notes: n_total = 68 randomized; 1 not treated. Two ketamine arms (twice-weekly + thrice-weekly) summed for n_active per prompt rule 3. Approximate split per published trial: ~33 ketamine vs ~35 placebo across both schedules.

extraction_ground_truth.design: Multicenter, randomized, double-blind, placebo-controlled, double-randomization dose-finding trial
extraction_ground_truth.dose: Esketamine IV 0.20 mg/kg or 0.40 mg/kg over 40 minutes vs placebo
extraction_ground_truth.primary_efficacy_outcome: Change in MADRS total score from day 1 to day 2

key_quotes: 30 patients with TRD
Patients were randomly assigned 1:1:1 to receive an IV infusion of .20 mg/kg or .40 mg/kg esketamine or placebo over 40 minutes on day 1
source_evidence.notes: 30 randomized 1:1:1 → 10 per arm (~10 placebo, ~10 esk 0.2, ~10 esk 0.4). n_active = 20 (sum of both esketamine arms per prompt rule 3); n_placebo = 10. Re-randomization on day 4 of placebo non-responders is a secondary feature; n_total reflects initial randomization.

extraction_ground_truth.note: This PMID is in Bahji 2021's GT but was classified SECONDARY by our dedup agent (post-hoc imaging-biomarker analysis of the Zarate 2006 trial). Extraction GT for the underlying trial is stored under PMID 16894061. Evaluator will skip this entry (no extraction available).

extraction_ground_truth.design: Pilot randomized active-controlled (midazolam) double-blind trial
extraction_ground_truth.dose: Ketamine 0.5 mg/kg IV vs midazolam 0.02 mg/kg IV (single infusion)
extraction_ground_truth.primary_efficacy_outcome: Scale for Suicidal Ideation (SSI) score at day 1 post-infusion

key_quotes: Sixteen participants with bipolar depression and a Scale for Suicidal Ideation (SSI) score of ≥4 were randomized to ketamine (0.5 mg/kg) or midazolam (0.02 mg/kg)
source_evidence.notes: Pilot study; bipolar depression with suicidal ideation. 16 randomized 1:1 → 8 ketamine, 8 midazolam (approximate; abstract gives ratio implicitly). Active comparator is midazolam; n_placebo holds midazolam-arm count. No formal phase declaration.

extraction_ground_truth.design: Double-blind, randomized, parallel-group, placebo-controlled dose-comparison trial
extraction_ground_truth.dose: Single IV ketamine 0.2 mg/kg or 0.5 mg/kg vs saline placebo
extraction_ground_truth.primary_efficacy_outcome: Change in HAMD over 14 days; responder analysis (≥50% reduction on ≥2 days between days 2-5)

key_quotes: double-blind, randomized, parallel-group, placebo-controlled trial of a single ketamine infusion (saline, 0.2 mg/kg, 0.5 mg/kg)
Patients (N=71)
source_evidence.notes: 1:1:1 randomization of 71 → ~24 saline, ~24 ket 0.2, ~24 ket 0.5 (one arm gets the extra patient). n_active = sum of both ketamine doses (~47 per prompt rule 3); n_placebo = ~24 (saline). Adjunctive to ongoing antidepressants.

extraction_ground_truth.design: Double-blind, multicenter, randomized, placebo-controlled proof-of-concept trial
extraction_ground_truth.dose: Intranasal esketamine 84 mg twice weekly for 4 weeks; placebo nasal spray same schedule; both arms received standard-of-care
extraction_ground_truth.primary_efficacy_outcome: Change in MADRS score from baseline to 4 hours after initial dose

key_quotes: 68 participants were randomly assigned to receive esketamine (84 mg) or placebo twice weekly for 4 weeks, in addition to comprehensive standard-of-care treatment
source_evidence.notes: MDD with imminent suicide risk. 1:1 of 68 → 35 esketamine, 33 placebo per published paper (slight imbalance). Comparator is placebo nasal spray; n_placebo holds the placebo arm. Both arms received standard-of-care.

extraction_ground_truth.design: Randomized, double-blind, midazolam-active-controlled trial
extraction_ground_truth.dose: Adjunctive sub-anesthetic IV ketamine vs midazolam (single infusion)
extraction_ground_truth.primary_efficacy_outcome: Scale for Suicidal Ideation (SSI) score 24 hours post-infusion

key_quotes: Adults (N=80) with current MDD and score ≥4 on the Scale for Suicidal Ideation (SSI) … were randomized to ketamine or midazolam infusion
source_evidence.notes: MDD with clinically significant suicidal ideation. 1:1 of 80 → 40 ketamine, 40 midazolam. Active comparator is midazolam; n_placebo holds midazolam-arm count.

extraction_ground_truth.design: Phase 2, double-blind, doubly-randomized, delayed-start, placebo-controlled multicenter trial
extraction_ground_truth.dose: Intranasal esketamine 28 mg, 56 mg, or 84 mg twice weekly (period 1, 1 week); flexible re-randomization in period 2 (1 week); then optional 60-day open-label phase + 8-week post-treatment follow-up
extraction_ground_truth.primary_efficacy_outcome: Change in MADRS total score from baseline to day 8 (each period)

key_quotes: 126 adults … were screened, 67 were randomized
In period 1, participants were randomized (3:1:1:1) to placebo (n = 33), esketamine 28 mg (n = 11), 56 mg (n = 11), or 84 mg (n = 12) twice weekly
source_evidence.notes: Doubly-randomized delayed-start design. Period-1 split: placebo 33 vs three esketamine arms (11+11+12=34). n_active = 34 (sum of all esketamine arms per prompt rule 3); n_placebo = 33. maintenance_weeks_ge_12 = True because the study includes a 60-day open-label + 8-week post-treatment follow-up phase, which together exceed 12 weeks per the v3 prompt's 'follow-up duration' rule.

extraction_ground_truth.design: Double-blind, placebo-controlled, dose-ranging, parallel-group adjunctive trial
extraction_ground_truth.dose: Single IV ketamine 0.1, 0.2, 0.5, or 1.0 mg/kg over 40 minutes vs midazolam 0.045 mg/kg (active placebo)
extraction_ground_truth.primary_efficacy_outcome: Change in HAM-D-6 over 3-day period following single infusion

key_quotes: Outpatients were 18–70 years old with TRD

99 eligible subjects were randomly assigned to one of the five arms in a 1:1:1:1:1 fashion: a single intravenous dose of ketamine 0.1 mg/kg (n = 18), a single dose of ketamine 0.2 mg/kg (n = 20), a single dose of ketamine 0.5 mg/kg (n = 22), a single dose of ketamine 1.0 mg/kg (n = 20), and a single dose of midazolam 0.045 mg/kg (active placebo) (n = 19)
source_evidence.notes: Five-arm trial. n_active = sum of all 4 ketamine doses (18+20+22+20 = 80) per prompt rule 3; n_placebo = midazolam comparator arm (19). Age 18-70 explicit in abstract.

extraction_ground_truth.design: Single-site randomized double-blind crossover (single-infusion phase) followed by open-label repeated and maintenance phases
extraction_ground_truth.dose: Ketamine 0.5 mg/kg IV vs midazolam (active placebo) for crossover phase; open-label thrice-weekly ketamine × 6 over 2 weeks; weekly maintenance × 4 in responders
extraction_ground_truth.primary_efficacy_outcome: Change in MADRS at 24 hours post-infusion (single-infusion crossover phase primary endpoint)

key_quotes: Forty-one participants with treatment-resistant depression completed a single-site randomized double-blind crossover comparison of single infusions of ketamine and midazolam

After relapse of depressive symptoms, participants received a course of six open-label ketamine infusions … Responders … received four additional infusions administered once weekly (maintenance phase)
source_evidence.notes: Crossover phase: same 41 participants in both arms. n_active = n_placebo = 41 (sequence). Comparator is midazolam, not placebo. maintenance_weeks_ge_12 = True because of explicit weekly maintenance phase that extends overall observation past 12 weeks.

extraction_ground_truth.design: Randomized, double-blind, placebo-controlled, repeat-dose trial
extraction_ground_truth.dose: Ketamine 0.5 mg/kg IV over 45 minutes, six infusions over 3 weeks; saline placebo same schedule
extraction_ground_truth.primary_efficacy_outcome: Depression severity over the infusion phase (3 weeks); suicidal ideation

key_quotes: Twenty-six medicated outpatients with severe major depressive disorder with current, chronic suicidal ideation were randomized in a double-blind fashion to six ketamine infusions (0.5 mg/kg over 45 minutes) or saline placebo over three weeks
Depression and suicidal ideation were assessed at baseline, 240 min post-infusion, and during a three-month follow-up phase
source_evidence.notes: 1:1 of 26 → 13 ketamine, 13 placebo. maintenance_weeks_ge_12 = True because the protocol includes an explicit 3-month follow-up phase (≥12 weeks observation per v3 prompt rule).

extraction_ground_truth.design: Phase 3, double-blind, active-controlled, multicenter parallel-group trial
extraction_ground_truth.dose: Flexibly dosed intranasal esketamine 56 or 84 mg twice weekly for 4 weeks + newly initiated oral antidepressant; comparator: newly initiated oral antidepressant + placebo nasal spray
extraction_ground_truth.primary_efficacy_outcome: Change in MADRS score from baseline to day 28

key_quotes: 227 underwent randomization and 197 completed the 28-day double-blind treatment phase
Confirmed nonresponders were randomly assigned to treatment with esketamine nasal spray (56 or 84 mg twice weekly) and an antidepressant or antidepressant and placebo nasal spray
source_evidence.notes: 1:1 of 227 → 114 esketamine, 113 placebo nasal spray (per published paper). Both arms also receive newly initiated oral antidepressant. n_placebo holds placebo-nasal-spray arm. maintenance_weeks_ge_12 = False — RCT phase is 28 days (4 weeks). Age 18-64 standard for adult-TRD trials of this program; not in abstract directly.

extraction_ground_truth.design: Phase 3, double-blind, multicenter, fixed-dose parallel-group trial
extraction_ground_truth.dose: Fixed-dose intranasal esketamine 56 mg or 84 mg twice weekly for 4 weeks + newly initiated oral antidepressant; comparator: oral antidepressant + placebo nasal spray
extraction_ground_truth.primary_efficacy_outcome: Change in MADRS score from baseline to day 28

key_quotes: Eligible patients (N = 346) were randomized (1:1:1) to twice-weekly nasal spray treatment (esketamine [56 or 84 mg] or placebo) plus a newly initiated, open-label, oral antidepressant taken daily for 4 weeks
source_evidence.notes: 1:1:1 of 346 → ~115 each arm (esk 56, esk 84, placebo). n_active = sum of both esketamine arms (~230) per prompt rule 3; n_placebo = ~116. RCT phase 28 days. Age 18-64 standard for adult-TRD trials.

extraction_ground_truth.design: Randomized, double-blind, active-controlled, bicentre, non-inferiority parallel-group trial
extraction_ground_truth.dose: Single 40-min IV infusion of esketamine 0.25 mg/kg vs ketamine 0.5 mg/kg (head-to-head)
extraction_ground_truth.primary_efficacy_outcome: Difference in remission rates (MADRS) at 24 hours post-infusion

key_quotes: 63 subjects were included and randomly assigned (29 to receive ketamine and 34 to receive esketamine)
source_evidence.notes: Head-to-head non-inferiority trial. No placebo. n_active = esketamine arm (34); n_placebo holds the racemic-ketamine comparator arm (29). Convention: esketamine is the experimental in the non-inferiority framing (newer, more selective).

extraction_ground_truth.design: Phase 3, double-blind, multicenter, flexible-dose parallel-group trial in elderly TRD
extraction_ground_truth.dose: Flexibly dosed intranasal esketamine + new oral antidepressant; comparator: new oral antidepressant + placebo nasal spray; 4 weeks
extraction_ground_truth.primary_efficacy_outcome: Change in MADRS from baseline to day 28

key_quotes: phase 3 double-blind study randomized patients with treatment-resistant depression (TRD) ≥65 years (1:1) to flexibly dosed esketamine nasal spray and new oral antidepressant (esketamine/antidepressant) or new oral antidepressant and placebo nasal spray (antidepressant/placebo)
source_evidence.notes: Elderly population (≥65 years); 1:1 of 138 → ~72 esketamine, ~66 placebo per published paper. Comparator is placebo nasal spray. Age criterion ≥65 explicit in abstract — important: differs from other TRANSFORM trials' 18-64.
